next_upgrades: Move AUREON_HNC_PACKET_MASTER_KEY and swarm agent keys out of .env and into Windows Credential Manager or an equivalent local secret store.
Use two-way swarm locknotes for high-value credentials and keep single-master HNC packets for lower-risk local bootstrap values.
Add key rotation: decrypt old hncqp1 tokens, re-encrypt with a new master key, and write a rotation evidence report.
Add optional KMS/HSM envelope mode for hosted or multi-user deployments.
Keep breaker checks in CI so tampered geometry, fragments, or AAD never decode.
Keep the symbolic route catalogs under audit so rune/star route seals stay reproducible and evidence-linked.

references.title: RFC 5869: HMAC-based Extract-and-Expand Key Derivation Function
NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 Rev. 5: Recommendation for Key Management
references.used_for: split knowledge, dual control, and key custody basis

rows.aureon_fit: bootstrap only; acceptable for local dev, weak for live credentials
rows.limitation: any process or user reading the file sees the secret

rows.aureon_fit: not acceptable for real credential protection
rows.limitation: encoding is reversible without a key

rows.aureon_fit: good for packet fingerprints, not for storing API keys Aureon must reuse
rows.integrity: detects equality when original is known
rows.limitation: cannot decrypt; API clients need the original secret

rows.aureon_fit: not preferred because tamper detection is easy to get wrong
rows.integrity: weak unless paired with a MAC
rows.limitation: malleability and padding risks without authenticated design

rows.aureon_fit: future transport upgrade; not required for local symmetric .env protection today
rows.limitation: does not remove the need for AEAD payload encryption and key custody

rows.aureon_fit: strong local upgrade for .env credential-at-rest protection and HNC decode proof
rows.integrity: packet hash, AES-GCM authentication, HNC geometry, and symbolic route seal
rows.limitation: security depends on keeping the master key outside git and away from leaked logs/process dumps
rows.tamper_detection: strong packet, geometry, symbolic route, AAD, and fragment checks

rows.aureon_fit: excellent baseline; HNC packet adds Aureon-specific authenticated context
rows.limitation: still only as strong as key storage and nonce discipline

rows.aureon_fit: best local HNC design for high-value secrets because a single agent/key cannot decode alone
rows.confidentiality: AES-GCM payload secrecy with two-agent key-share reconstruction
rows.integrity: packet hash, AES-GCM authentication, locknote authentication, HNC geometry, and symbolic route contract
rows.key_management: two independent agent master keys required per decode
rows.limitation: still depends on keeping agent master keys separate and protected outside git/logs/process dumps
rows.method: Aureon HNC swarm two-way locknote packet v1
rows.tamper_detection: strong packet, locknote, AAD, geometry, symbolic route, and missing-pair checks

rows.aureon_fit: best next local upgrade: store the HNC master key outside .env
best production/server target when multiple users or hosted SaaS are involved
rows.confidentiality: strong with hardware or managed key custody
rows.limitation: requires external account, permissions, cost, and network availability

security_boundary.cryptographic_secret_role: AES-GCM payload secrecy under a master key or two-agent swarm key-share reconstruction
security_boundary.hnc_geometry_role: authenticated decode contract and evidence context
security_boundary.swarm_locknote_role: split-knowledge dual-control gate where one agent key cannot decode alone
security_boundary.symbolic_route_role: authenticated rune/star/Maeshowe context seal; proves route intent and catalog provenance but is not secret key material
security_boundary.what_hnc_does_not_replace: key custody, OS permissions, KMS/HSM policy, endpoint authentication, and process memory protection

summary.top_recommendation: store HNC/swarm agent keys in Windows Credential Manager or another OS secret store, keep only hncqp1 packets and public locknotes in .env/state, and require two agent keys for high-value decode
